title: ग्रह पीड़ा हर स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री ग्रह पीड़ा हर स्तोत्रम् ॥

ब्रह्मा मुरारिस्त्रिपुरान्तकारी भानुः शशी भूमिसुतो बुधश्च।
गुरुश्च शुक्रः शनिराहुकेतवः सर्वे ग्रहाः शान्तिकराः भवन्तु॥१॥

सूर्यो रक्तवर्णः शशी श्वेतवर्णः मंगलो रक्तः बुधो हरितवर्णः।
गुरुः पीतवर्णः शुक्रः श्वेतवर्णः शनिर्नीलवर्णः सर्वग्रहाः प्रसीदन्तु॥२॥

आदित्याय सोमाय मंगलाय बुधाय च।
गुरुशुक्रशनिभ्यश्च राहवे केतवे नमः॥३॥

सर्वेषां ग्रहदोषाणां शान्तिकारणमुत्तमम्।
नवग्रहान् प्रणम्यादौ सर्वपीडानिवारणम्॥४॥

यः कोऽपि ग्रहपीडार्तो यत्किंचित् दोषपीडितः।
इदं स्तोत्रं पठेद्भक्त्या सर्वपीडा विनश्यति॥५॥

सर्वग्रहमहादोषनाशनं सर्वकामदम्।
सर्वपीडाहरं दिव्यं स्तोत्रमेतत्सदा पठेत्॥६॥

॥ इति श्री ग्रह पीड़ा हर स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Graha Peeda Hara Stotram ||

Brahma Muraris-tripurantakari Bhanuh Shashi Bhumi-suto Budhash-cha.
Gurush-cha Shukrah Shani-rahu-ketavah Sarve Grahah Shanti-karah Bhavantu.||1||

Suryo Rakta-varnah Shashi Shveta-varnah Mangalo Raktah Budho Harita-varnah.
Guruh Pita-varnah Shukrah Shveta-varnah Shanir-nila-varnah Sarva-grahah Prasidantu.||2||

Adityaya Somaya Mangalaya Budhaya Cha.
Guru-shukra-shanibhyash-cha Rahave Ketave Namah.||3||

Sarvesham Graha-doshanam Shanti-karanam-uttamam.
Nava-grahan Pranamya-adau Sarva-pida-nivaranam.||4||

Yah Ko-api Graha-pidarto Yat-kinchit Dosha-piditah.
Idam Stotram Pathed-bhaktya Sarva-pida Vinashyati.||5||

Sarva-graha-maha-dosha-nashanam Sarva-kama-dam.
Sarva-pida-haram Divyam Stotram-etat-sada Pathet.||6||

|| Iti Shri Graha Peeda Hara Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning: ब्रह्मा, विष्णु, शिव — सूर्य, चन्द्र, मंगल, बुध, गुरु, शुक्र, शनि, राहु, केतु — सभी ग्रह शान्तिकारक हों। सूर्य रक्तवर्ण, चन्द्र श्वेत, मंगल रक्त, बुध हरित, गुरु पीत, शुक्र श्वेत, शनि नील — सभी ग्रह प्रसन्न हों। नवग्रहों को प्रणाम कर सर्वपीड़ा निवारण। जो कोई ग्रहपीड़ा से पीड़ित हो, इस स्तोत्र को भक्ति से पढ़े तो सर्वपीड़ा नष्ट होती है।
benefits: Universal prayer to pacify all nine planets simultaneously
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of types of graha dosha and planetary afflictions
Invokes relief during difficult dasha and antardasha periods
Traditionally recited for fulfillment of desires through navagraha grace
Simple yet powerful stotra for daily planetary pacification
Ideal for those experiencing multiple planetary afflictions
description: Dedicated to Sun God Surya, the Graha Peeda Hara Stotram (ग्रह पीड़ा हर स्तोत्रम्) is a devotional hymn of praise revered in Hindu tradition. This sacred composition is sourced from Jyotish Shastra and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Devotees recite this prayer to grant relief during difficult dasha and antardasha periods and remove all types of graha dosha and planetary afflictions. It is also said to simple yet powerful stotra for daily planetary pacification. This sacred text is ideally recited on Sunday (Ravivar) and Saturday (Shanivar) for enhanced blessings. The text contains 6 verses and takes around 3 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Graha Peeda Hara Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Graha Peeda Hara Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The Graha Peeda Hara Stotram (ग्रह पीड़ा हर स्तोत्रम् — literally Stotra that Removes Planetary Affliction) is a compact six-verse Sanskrit hymn for the simultaneous pacification (shanti) of all nine planets (Navagraha) — Surya (Sun), Soma/Chandra (Moon), Mangala (Mars), Budha (Mercury), Guru/Brihaspati (Jupiter), Shukra (Venus), Shani (Saturn), Rahu (north lunar node) and Ketu (south lunar node). The opening verse uniquely invokes the Trimurti (Brahma the creator, Murari/Vishnu, Tripurantakari/Shiva) alongside the navagraha to ensure their combined shanti-power. The second verse establishes the iconographic colours of the seven main planets — Surya red, Chandra white, Mangala red, Budha green, Guru yellow, Shukra white, Shani blue — used in jyotish-yantra worship. The third verse contains the canonical navagraha-namaskara mantra Adityaya Somaya Mangalaya Budhaya cha, Guru-Shukra-Shanibhyash-cha Rahave Ketave namah. The phala-shruti promises destruction of all graha-pidas. (Source: Jyotish Shastra navagraha shanti tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: Who are the nine planets (Navagraha) addressed in this stotra?
seoFaqs.answer: The Navagraha — nine cosmic graha-devatas in Vedic jyotish — are: (1) Surya / Aditya — the Sun, lord of soul, father, leadership, vitality, presiding over Sunday, ruby gemstone, red colour; (2) Soma / Chandra — the Moon, lord of mind, mother, emotions, presiding over Monday, pearl, white; (3) Mangala / Kuja — Mars, lord of energy, brothers, courage, presiding over Tuesday, coral, red; (4) Budha — Mercury, lord of intellect, communication, business, presiding over Wednesday, emerald, green; (5) Brihaspati / Guru — Jupiter, lord of wisdom, dharma, children, marriage, presiding over Thursday, yellow sapphire, yellow; (6) Shukra — Venus, lord of love, marriage, arts, presiding over Friday, diamond, white; (7) Shani — Saturn, lord of karma, longevity, discipline, presiding over Saturday, blue sapphire, blue; (8) Rahu — north lunar node, the chhaya-graha of obsession and unconventional desires, hessonite garnet; (9) Ketu — south lunar node, the chhaya-graha of detachment and moksha, cat's eye. The stotra invokes all nine for combined pacification. (Sources: Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra; Jataka Parijata; Phaladeepika.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Graha Peeda Hara Stotram be recited for maximum benefit?
seoFaqs.answer: The Graha Peeda Hara Stotram is most powerful on Sunday (Ravivar — Surya), Saturday (Shanivar — Shani, the most malefic planet requiring greatest pacification), all Sankranti days (especially Makara Sankranti when Surya enters Capricorn), Amavasya (no-moon day, especially Mauni Amavasya and Pitru Paksha Amavasya for ancestor-graha pacification), Eclipse days (Surya Grahana solar eclipse and Chandra Grahana lunar eclipse — supreme times for graha shanti), and at the start of every grahantara (planetary transit), during difficult Mahadasha or Antardasha periods of malefics (especially Shani, Rahu, Ketu), during Shani Sade Sati (the seven-and-half year Saturn-transit through 12th-1st-2nd from natal Moon), Shani Dhaiyya, and during Pitru Dosha or Kala Sarpa Dosha. Daily morning recitation facing east is the standard remedial prescription of jyotishis. (Sources: Jyotish remedial paddhati; Drikpanchang shanti days.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the colours of the nine planets mentioned in the stotra?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 2 of the Graha Peeda Hara Stotram specifies the canonical iconographic colours of the seven main planets used in Navagraha mandala-puja, yantra-pratishtha and gemstone remediation: (1) Surya (Sun) — rakta-varnah, red, gemstone manikya (ruby), copper metal, Sunday; (2) Chandra (Moon) — shveta-varnah, white, gemstone mukta (pearl), silver metal, Monday; (3) Mangala (Mars) — rakta, red, gemstone praval (red coral), copper, Tuesday; (4) Budha (Mercury) — harita-varnah, green, gemstone marakata (emerald), brass, Wednesday; (5) Guru (Jupiter) — pita-varnah, yellow, gemstone pushpa-raga (yellow sapphire), gold, Thursday; (6) Shukra (Venus) — shveta-varnah, white, gemstone vajra (diamond), silver, Friday; (7) Shani (Saturn) — nila-varnah, blue, gemstone nilamani (blue sapphire), iron, Saturday. Rahu and Ketu (chhaya-grahas) take the colours smoke-grey and parrot-green respectively. These colours guide cloth offerings, flower selection and gemstone-recommendation in jyotish remediation. (Sources: Brihat Samhita of Varahamihira; Jyotish Tantra colour-charts.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Graha Peeda Hara Stotram grant according to tradition?
seoFaqs.answer: The phala-shruti promises the following blessings to sincere recitation: (1) sarva-pida vinashyati — destruction of every form of graha-pida (planetary affliction), (2) sarva-graha-maha-dosha-nashanam — removal of all major navagraha doshas (Manglik dosha, Kala Sarpa Dosha, Pitru Dosha, Shani Sade Sati, Shrapit Yoga, etc.), (3) sarva-kama-dam — fulfilment of all worldly desires that were blocked by malefic planetary positions, (4) shanti-karah bhavantu — pacification of all difficult Mahadasha and Antardasha periods, (5) protection during eclipses, planetary transits, sandhi-kala (transition periods), and Bhrigu-bindu activations, (6) blessings of the Trimurti (Brahma-Vishnu-Shiva) alongside the navagraha — a unique combination not found in most navagraha stotras, (7) suitability as a daily prayer for those with multiple planetary afflictions, weak ascendant lords, or compromised navamsa charts, and (8) general health, prosperity and protection. (Source: Graha Peeda Hara Stotram phala-shruti.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the difference between the Graha Peeda Hara Stotram and the Navagraha Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: Both are navagraha-shanti texts but differ in author, length and format. The Navagraha Stotram (Japa Kusuma Sankasham Surya, etc.) is the universally famous nine-verse stotra traditionally attributed to Maharishi Vyasa — one verse for each graha, in classical anushtubh metre, describing the iconography (Surya as red as the japa flower, Chandra white as curd, Mangala born of the earth Bhumi-suta, etc.) and ending with namami (I bow to). It is recited at every Navagraha shanti homa and is the standard navagraha temple prayer at the Navagraha Sthala temples of Tamil Nadu (Suryanar Koil, Thingalur, Vaitheeshwaran Koil, Thiruvenkadu, Alangudi, Kanjanur, Thirunallar, Thirunageshwaram, Keezhperumpallam). The Graha Peeda Hara Stotram is a shorter compositional variant (six verses) that uniquely invokes the Trimurti alongside the Navagraha and emphasises the graha-colour iconography for yantra-puja. Many jyotishis recite both in sequence. (Source: Navagraha shanti paddhati.)
